parsed_review.summary: CP00-522 is a clean descriptor-only slice covering the 10 planned RP17 units RP17.P02.M05.S21-RP17.P02.M06.S05 (M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail x5, M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head x5). The contract block (p02_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor, current_pack, cp522_requirements/no_write_attestation, historical_pack_bindings tail), registry pack binding + requirements + no-write attestation, service constructors, validators, the rp17 validation script (deep-equality of contract vs registry constants), tests, README anchor, and the live closeout-pack-plan entry are all mutually consistent. Source inspection (not summary-only, as required by security_audit + review deliverables present) confirms the changed code is pure frozen-object construction and structural validation: no fs/network/DB writes, no real client/matter data, no runtime dispatch, no leak surface. Boundaries hold: descriptor_only true, runtime_execution false, no_write_attestation flags all false for writes/dispatch, omits_unauthorized_data true, customer_safe_errors_only true. Authority boundary is correct - manifest production_ready:false and runtime_ready:false, production_ready_flag is descriptor-verified (not a runtime/enterprise-trust claim), claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false, Claude is read-only and not final approver (enforced by validator errors if promotes_claude_to_final_approval !== false), human approval gated to CP00-523. Hermes is used only as deterministic evidence/receipt labels (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false). Review hardening is healthy: readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON), review-request pins claude-opus-4-8/max/read_only/Read,Grep,Glob, and command-evidence shows all implementation/test/contract/plan/repo validations at exit 0. Two non-blocking P3 metadata notes only.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies the pack's own dir and handoff doc as both implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-522/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in BOTH untracked_implementation_files (lines 151-154) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 158-159). A path cannot be both an active pack implementation artifact and an unrelated preserved file. This also diverges from the authoritative manifest worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest lines 549-560), which correctly excludes both paths. No functional impact on review scope: the authoritative active_pack_implementation_refs list and the manifest are correct and the pack's files are fully accounted for, so the contradictory labeling does not cause any pack file to be missed or any unrelated file to be pulled in.
findings.recommendation: Regenerate changed-file-scope.json so unrelated_dirty_files_preserved excludes the active pack directory and the handoff doc, matching the manifest's worktree_transition_policy list.

findings.title: Adjudication and construction-inspection artifacts not yet present (expected at this stage)
findings.details: manifest required_pack_artifacts/evidence_refs list claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json (lines 502-515), but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist on disk. This is the correct ordering: manifest hardening_sequence (line 568) places read_only_claude_run_raw_capture before finding_adjudication and construction_inspection, and command-evidence notes (line 127) state the closeout-pack:validate / receipt-validate gates run post-review before commit. Recorded only for transparency that these downstream artifacts were verified as pending-by-design, not missing-in-error.
findings.recommendation: Proceed with the remaining hardening sequence (normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final closeout validation) and ensure closeout-pack:validate confirms all required_pack_artifacts exist before commit.
